proposed_relations.description: Offset bus lanes on Webster Avenue are part of the Webster Avenue SBS (Bx41) project

evidence_refs.source_quote: ■ Bx41 LTD → Bx41 SBS ■ 4 miles of offset bus lanes with bus bulb stations ■ For the entire route: – Off-board fare collection – Low-floor buses – Station and bus branding – Transit signal priority – Pedestrian safety improvements
▪ Replace one general travel lane with a bus lane in each direction

decisions.rationale: p011_c0002 explicitly states the Bx41 SBS project includes 4 miles of offset bus lanes; p024_c0003 confirms replacing one general travel lane with a bus lane in each direction on Webster Avenue.

proposed_relations.description: Off-board fare collection is part of the Webster Avenue SBS (Bx41) project

evidence_refs.source_quote: 1. Before you board, dip your MetroCard at the MetroCard machine or insert coins into the coin machine located on the sidewalk 2. Take your proof of payment receipt 3. Enter through the front or rear door of bus - no need to show the receipt to the driver
▪ NYCT inspector teams conduct random checks of buses ▪ $100 fine for not having a receipt ▪ Fare evasion declined on the Bx12 and M15 after pre-payment was introduced

decisions.rationale: p012_c0003 describes the off-board fare payment process for the Bx41 SBS; p012_c0007 describes enforcement via NYCT inspectors. Both are from the Webster Avenue SBS presentation.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 1. Before you board, dip your MetroCard at the MetroCard machine or insert coins into the coin machine located on the sidewalk 2. Take your proof of payment receipt 3. Enter through the front or rear door of bus - no need to show the receipt to the driver
▪ NYCT inspector teams conduct random checks of buses ▪ $100 fine for not having a receipt ▪ Fare evasion declined on the Bx12 and M15 after pre-payment was introduced

proposed_relations.description: Pedestrian safety improvements are part of the Webster Avenue SBS (Bx41) project

evidence_refs.source_quote: The image shows a wide-angle view of a city street intersection, likely in New York City, with several callout boxes pointing to proposed design features. The callout boxes are as follows: Pedestrian neckdowns : Points to a narrow pedestrian crossing area on the left side of the street. SBS Station / Bus Bulb : Points to a bus stop area with a shelter and a bus. Pedestrian refuge islands and greening : Points to a small green island with a tree in the middle of the intersection. Transit Signal Priority : Points to a traffic signal pole on the right side of the street. Left-turn Bay : Points to a designated area for left-turning vehicles on the right side of the street. Offset bus lanes : Points to a bus lane on the left side of the street, marked with a red surface. The street is labeled "Webster Avenue / E 167th Street" in the bottom right corner.
4. Curb extensions and medians improve pedestrian safety 5. Addresses speeding and vehicle safety issues 6. Maintains appropriate traffic flows and circulation

▪ SBS stations at existing LTD stops ▪ Relocate Bx40/42 and Bx36 bus stops to Valentine Av ▪ Create pedestrian areas with temporary paint treatment ▪ New midblock pedestrian crossings ▪ Extend median on Webster Av

decisions.rationale: p016_c0002 shows pedestrian neckdowns, refuge islands as part of Webster Ave proposed design; p018_c0002 states curb extensions and medians improve pedestrian safety; p033_c0003 describes pedestrian areas with paint treatment at E Tremont. All are within the Webster Avenue SBS project.
